text:
Once upon a time in a bustling forest town, there lived a curious little fox named Fergus. Fergus loved to explore the woods at dawn and dusk. One day he stumbled upon a hidden glen unlike any he'd ever seen.

There, among the towering oaks, grew an elven orchard. The apples were as shiny as gemstones, and the cherries were the deepest, brightest purps. Fergus sat on an old branch, devouring an apple so big he could barely chew it. He knew there was no way he could ever reach those apples with his small fox claws. Something extraordinary seemed to call to him.

He rolled a rocky boulder closer. It took all his strength, but eventually it rested just beside an orange berry.

With a giggle that sounded a bit silly for a wise old fox, he plucked the berry.

Moments later, a cheerful fairy fluttered down from a birch tree. "What delightful fruit!" she chirped brightly
